summary: ByteDance Seed surfaced the clearest product artifact of the day with Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and a new Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM aimed at real-time, interruption-tolerant voice interaction. Zhipu AI expanded its Intel partnership to put Qingyan and CodeGeeX on AI PCs, while new research on transformer hallucinations and billion-parameter interatomic potentials pointed to potentially important advances in model reliability and scientific AI infrastructure.

top_topics.title: Seed 2.0 Official Launch
top_topics.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The release introduces a new version of the company's foundation model lineup.

items.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment Opens
items.summary: ByteDance Seed announced that its 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment program is now open, including internship opportunities.

items.title: Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena
items.summary: ByteDance Seed released the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview model on Arena, marking a preview availability of the new model.

items.title: Introducing Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
items.summary: ByteDance Seed announced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a speech model focused on attentive listening, robust interference suppression, and more natural real-time interaction.

items.title: Seed 2.0 Official Launch
items.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0, introducing the new version of its foundation model lineup.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The release introduces a new version of the company's foundation model lineup.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: ByteDance is signaling continued investment in both frontier multimodal models and voice interfaces, which matters for competitors tracking Chinese model capabilities and enterprise buyers evaluating real-time speech stacks.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM Debut from ByteDance Seed
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: ByteDance Seed released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, giving the market a named preview artifact rather than a vague roadmap announcement.

Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM was introduced with emphasis on attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural real-time interaction, pointing to a push beyond turn-based voice assistants.

The same announcement stream also opened ByteDance Seed's 2027 foundation-model campus recruiting program, a signal that the company is still scaling long-horizon research capacity alongside productization.

top_topics.title: Zhipu AI and Intel deepen AI PC partnership
top_topics.summary: Zhipu AI announced expanded cooperation with Intel at the Intel AI PC ecosystem event. The partnership delivered on-device versions of Zhipu Qingyan and the CodeGeeX AI coding assistant for AI PCs, alongside other end-cloud integrated AI PC innovations.

items.title: Zhipu AI and Intel deepen AI PC partnership
items.summary: Zhipu AI announced expanded cooperation with Intel at the Intel AI PC ecosystem event. The partnership delivered on-device versions of Zhipu Qingyan and the CodeGeeX AI coding assistant for AI PCs, alongside other end-cloud integrated AI PC innovations.

items.title: Zhipu AI publishes its first earnings report notice
items.summary: Zhipu AI announced arrangements for its 2025 annual results release, stating it will publish full-year results for the period ended December 31, 2025 on March 31, 2026. The notice identifies the company with Hong Kong stock code 02513.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Zhipu AI announced expanded cooperation with Intel at the Intel AI PC ecosystem event. The partnership delivered on-device versions of Zhipu Qingyan and the CodeGeeX AI coding assistant for AI PCs, alongside other end-cloud integrated AI PC innovations.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: This is a concrete distribution move for on-device AI in China, relevant to OEMs, chip vendors, and software leaders deciding where local inference and AI PC demand may consolidate.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Zhipu Qingyan and CodeGeeX Expand to Intel AI PCs in Deeper Zhipu AI Partnership
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: Zhipu AI said its cooperation with Intel now includes on-device versions of Zhipu Qingyan and the CodeGeeX coding assistant for AI PCs.
The partnership was presented at an Intel AI PC ecosystem event, indicating Intel is using named application partners rather than only generic platform claims to support its AI PC strategy.

Zhipu AI also disclosed plans to publish full-year 2025 results on March 31, 2026, with the notice identifying Hong Kong stock code 02513, adding public-market visibility to a major Chinese model company.

top_topics.title: Hallucination as Trajectory Commitment: Causal Evidence for Asymmetric Attractor Dynamics in Transformer Generation
top_topics.summary: We present causal evidence that hallucination in autoregressive language models is an early trajectory commitment governed by asymmetric attractor dynamics. Using same-prompt bifurcation, in which we repeatedly sample identical inputs to observe spontaneous divergence, we isolate trajectory dynamics from prompt-level confounds. On Qwen2.5-1.5B across 61 prompts spanning six categories, 27 prompts (44.3%) bifurcate with factual and hallucinated trajectories diverging at the first generated token (KL = 0 at step 0, KL > 1.0 at step 1). Activation patching across 28 layers reveals a pronounced...

items.title: Hallucination as Trajectory Commitment: Causal Evidence for Asymmetric Attractor Dynamics in Transformer Generation
items.summary: We present causal evidence that hallucination in autoregressive language models is an early trajectory commitment governed by asymmetric attractor dynamics. Using same-prompt bifurcation, in which we repeatedly sample identical inputs to observe spontaneous divergence, we isolate trajectory dynamics from prompt-level confounds. On Qwen2.5-1.5B across 61 prompts spanning six categories, 27 prompts (44.3%) bifurcate with factual and hallucinated trajectories diverging at the first generated token (KL = 0 at step 0, KL > 1.0 at step 1). Activation patching across 28 layers reveals a pronounced...

metadata.comment: Gives causal evidence that hallucination arises from asymmetric attractor dynamics and early trajectory commitment in transformer generation.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: We present causal evidence that hallucination in autoregressive language models is an early trajectory commitment governed by asymmetric attractor dynamics. Using same-prompt bifurcation, in which we repeatedly sample identical inputs to observe spontaneous divergence, we isolate trajectory dynamics from prompt-level confounds. On Qwen2.5-1.5B across 61 prompts spanning six categories, 27 prompts (44.3%) bifurcate with factual and hallucinated trajectories diverging at the first generated token (KL = 0 at step 0, KL > 1.0 at step 1). Activation patching across 28 layers reveals a pronounced...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: If validated, this work could shift hallucination mitigation from post-hoc filtering toward earlier intervention in generation dynamics, affecting model safety, eval design, and inference control.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Hallucination as Trajectory Commitment Paper Offers Causal Mechanism for Transformer Errors
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The paper argues hallucinations arise from early trajectory commitment in autoregressive generation, framed as asymmetric attractor dynamics rather than only missing knowledge or weak decoding.
Its core method is same-prompt bifurcation: repeatedly sampling identical inputs to observe spontaneous divergence and isolate when generations commit to correct versus hallucinatory paths.

The contribution is causal in framing, not just descriptive, which makes it more strategically relevant for teams building decoding controls, interpretability tools, or reliability-focused model products.

top_topics.title: Breaking the Training Barrier of Billion-Parameter Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
top_topics.summary: Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials (uMLIPs), pre-trained on massively diverse datasets encompassing inorganic materials and organic molecules across the entire periodic table, serve as foundational models for quantum-accurate physical simulations. However, uMLIP training requires second-order derivatives, which lack corresponding parallel training frameworks; moreover, scaling to the billion-parameter regime causes explosive growth in computation and communication overhead, making its training a tremendous challenge. We introduce MatRIS-MoE, a billion-parameter Mixture-of-Exp...

items.title: Breaking the Training Barrier of Billion-Parameter Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
items.summary: Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials (uMLIPs), pre-trained on massively diverse datasets encompassing inorganic materials and organic molecules across the entire periodic table, serve as foundational models for quantum-accurate physical simulations. However, uMLIP training requires second-order derivatives, which lack corresponding parallel training frameworks; moreover, scaling to the billion-parameter regime causes explosive growth in computation and communication overhead, making its training a tremendous challenge. We introduce MatRIS-MoE, a billion-parameter Mixture-of-Exp...

metadata.comment: Introduces a high-dimensional distributed training framework for billion-parameter MoE interatomic models, overcoming second-derivative and exascale communication bottlenecks.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials (uMLIPs), pre-trained on massively diverse datasets encompassing inorganic materials and organic molecules across the entire periodic table, serve as foundational models for quantum-accurate physical simulations. However, uMLIP training requires second-order derivatives, which lack corresponding parallel training frameworks; moreover, scaling to the billion-parameter regime causes explosive growth in computation and communication overhead, making its training a tremendous challenge. We introduce MatRIS-MoE, a billion-parameter Mixture-of-Exp...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: This is the strongest science-facing infrastructure artifact in the set, with implications for materials discovery, molecular simulation, and the compute economics of foundation models for physics.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Breaking the Training Barrier of Billion-Parameter Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials Targets Scientific AI Scale-Up
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The paper focuses on billion-parameter universal machine learning interatomic potentials, or uMLIPs, designed as foundation models for quantum-accurate physical simulation across inorganic materials and organic molecules.
Its central claim is that it addresses the training bottleneck that has limited scaling of these models, a key constraint for labs and startups trying to build general-purpose scientific simulators.

The authors position uMLIPs as pre-trained on highly diverse datasets spanning the periodic table, which, if borne out, would make them more analogous to general foundation models than narrow chemistry predictors.

top_topics.title: Photonic AI: A Hybrid Diffractive Holographic Neural System for Passive Optical Real-Time Image Classification
top_topics.summary: A paper titled "Photonic AI: A Hybrid Diffractive Holographic Neural System for Passive Optical Real-Time Image Classification" was highlighted in the daily hotspot feed. It describes a hybrid optical neural system for real-time image classification.

items.title: Photonic AI: A Hybrid Diffractive Holographic Neural System for Passive Optical Real-Time Image Classification
items.summary: It proposes a genuinely different physical substrate and architectural framing for neural inference, suggesting a new frontier beyond standard electronic compute.

metadata.comment: Introduces an operator-theoretic neural architecture that maps learned phase patterns to physically realizable passive optical computation.
metadata.spotlight_comment: It proposes a genuinely different physical substrate and architectural framing for neural inference, suggesting a new frontier beyond standard electronic compute.

topics.title: Headless everything for personal AI
topics.summary:
Headless everything for personal AI

Matt Webb thinks headless services are about to become much more common:

Why? Because using personal AIs is a better experience for users than using services directly (honestly); and headless services are quicker and more dependable for the personal AIs than having them click round a GUI with a bot-controlled mouse.

Evidently Marc Benioff thinks so too:

Welcome Salesforce Headless 360: No Browser Required! Our API is the UI. Entire Salesforce & Agentforce & Slack platforms are now exposed as APIs, MCP, & CL...

topics.title: Cloud development platform Vercel was hacked
topics.summary: Vercel, a major development platform that hosts and deploys web apps, was compromised, and the hackers are attempting to sell stolen data. A person claiming to be a member of ShinyHunters, which was behind the recent hack of Rockstar Games, posted some data online, including employee names, email addresses, and activity time stamps. Vercel confirmed […]

topics.title: Claude Token Counter, now with model comparisons
topics.summary: Simon Willison updated the Claude Token Counter tool to add model comparisons. The tool helps users compare token counts across Claude models.

items.title: Claude Token Counter, now with model comparisons
items.summary:
Claude Token Counter, now with model comparisons

I upgraded my Claude Token Counter tool to add the ability to run the same count against different models in order to compare them.

As far as I can tell Claude Opus 4.7 is the first model to change the tokenizer, so it's only worth running comparisons between 4.7 and 4.6. The Claude token counting API accepts any Claude model ID though so I've included options for all four of the notable current models (Opus 4.7 and 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, and Haiku 4.5).

In the Opus 4.7 announcement

topics.title: Closing the Theory-Practice Gap in Spiking Transformers via Effective Dimension
topics.summary: Spiking transformers achieve competitive accuracy with conventional transformers while offering $38$-$57\times$ energy efficiency on neuromorphic hardware, yet no theoretical framework guides their design. This paper establishes the first comprehensive expressivity theory for spiking self-attention. We prove that spiking attention with Leaky Integrate-and-Fire neurons is a universal approximator of continuous permutation-equivariant functions, providing explicit spike circuit constructions including a novel lateral inhibition network for softmax normalization with proven $O(1/\sqrt{T})$ con...

topics.title: Collective Kernel EFT for Pre-activation ResNets
topics.summary: In finite-width deep neural networks, the empirical kernel $G$ evolves stochastically across layers. We develop a collective kernel effective field theory (EFT) for pre-activation ResNets based on a $G$-only closure hierarchy and diagnose its finite validity window. Exploiting the exact conditional Gaussianity of residual increments, we derive an exact stochastic recursion for $G$. Applying Gaussian approximations systematically yields a continuous-depth ODE system for the mean kernel $K_0$, the kernel covariance $V_4$, and the $1/n$ mean correction $K_{1,\mathrm{EFT}}$, which emerges diagr...

items.title: Collective Kernel EFT for Pre-activation ResNets
items.summary: In finite-width deep neural networks, the empirical kernel $G$ evolves stochastically across layers. We develop a collective kernel effective field theory (EFT) for pre-activation ResNets based on a $G$-only closure hierarchy and diagnose its finite validity window. Exploiting the exact conditional Gaussianity of residual increments, we derive an exact stochastic recursion for $G$. Applying Gaussian approximations systematically yields a continuous-depth ODE system for the mean kernel $K_0$, the kernel covariance $V_4$, and the $1/n$ mean correction $K_{1,\mathrm{EFT}}$, which emerges diagr...

metadata.comment: Finite-width ResNet theory via a collective-kernel EFT that derives stochastic kernel recursions and pinpoints where G-only closure breaks.

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: In finite-width deep neural networks, the empirical kernel $G$ evolves stochastically across layers. We develop a collective kernel effective field theory (EFT) for pre-activation ResNets based on a $G$-only closure hierarchy and diagnose its finite validity window. Exploiting the exact conditional Gaussianity of residual increments, we derive an exact stochastic recursion for $G$. Applying Gaussian approximations systematically yields a continuous-depth ODE system for the mean kernel $K_0$, the kernel covariance $V_4$, and the $1/n$ mean correction $K_{1,\mathrm{EFT}}$, which emerges diagr...
topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: This research paper surfaced across 1 independent source(s).
topics.HEADLINE: Collective Kernel EFT for Pre-activation ResNets

topics.title: Faster LLM Inference via Sequential Monte Carlo
topics.summary: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates language model inference by drafting tokens from a cheap proposal model and verifying them against an expensive target model via rejection sampling. Because rejection truncates the draft block at the first error, throughput degrades when draft and target diverge. Rather than rejecting draft tokens outright, we propose to reweight them. To this end, we introduce sequential Monte Carlo speculative decoding (SMC-SD), which replaces token-level rejection with importance-weighted resampling over a population of draft particles. SMC-SD is a principled approxi...

topics.title: Geometric regularization of autoencoders via observed stochastic dynamics
topics.summary: Stochastic dynamical systems with slow or metastable behavior evolve, on long time scales, on an unknown low-dimensional manifold in high-dimensional ambient space. Building a reduced simulator from short-burst ambient ensembles is a long-standing problem: local-chart methods like ATLAS suffer from exponential landmark scaling and per-step reprojection, while autoencoder alternatives leave tangent-bundle geometry poorly constrained, and the errors propagate into the learned drift and diffusion. We observe that the ambient covariance~$\Lambda$ already encodes coordinate-invariant tangent-spa...

topics.title: Neural Continuous-Time Markov Chain: Discrete Diffusion via Decoupled Jump Timing and Direction
topics.summary: Discrete diffusion models based on continuous-time Markov chains (CTMCs) have shown strong performance on language and discrete data generation, yet existing approaches typically parameterize the reverse rate matrix as a single object -- via concrete scores, clean-data predictions ($x_0$-parameterization), or denoising distributions -- rather than aligning the parameterization with the intrinsic CTMC decomposition into jump timing and jump direction. Since a CTMC is fundamentally a Poisson process fully determined by these two quantities, decomposing along this structure is closer to first...

paper_spotlight.label: New Frontier Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that appear to open a genuinely new direction, paradigm, or field.

items.title: Sequential KV Cache Compression via Probabilistic Language Tries: Beyond the Per-Vector Shannon Limit
items.summary: The paper proposes an unusually ambitious new framing of KV-cache compression at the sequence level, potentially opening a new compression direction even if still speculative.
items.spotlight_comment: The paper proposes an unusually ambitious new framing of KV-cache compression at the sequence level, potentially opening a new compression direction even if still speculative.

items.title: A Wasserstein Geometric Framework for Hebbian Plasticity
items.summary: This proposes a highly unusual geometric foundation for memory and plasticity that feels like a genuinely new conceptual direction.
items.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
items.spotlight_comment: This proposes a highly unusual geometric foundation for memory and plasticity that feels like a genuinely new conceptual direction.

items.title: (1D) Ordered Tokens Enable Efficient Test-Time Search
items.summary: It proposes a potentially new design axis—token structure as a lever for test-time scaling/search—not just a routine benchmark improvement.
items.spotlight_comment: It proposes a potentially new design axis—token structure as a lever for test-time scaling/search—not just a routine benchmark improvement.

items.title: Photonic AI: A Hybrid Diffractive Holographic Neural System for Passive Optical Real-Time Image Classification
items.summary: It proposes a genuinely different physical substrate and architectural framing for neural inference, suggesting a new frontier beyond standard electronic compute.
items.spotlight_comment: It proposes a genuinely different physical substrate and architectural framing for neural inference, suggesting a new frontier beyond standard electronic compute.

paper_spotlight.label: Daily Hot Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that feel broadly important to the day and belong in the hotspot paper feed.

items.title: The Illusion of Equivalence: Systematic FP16 Divergence in KV-Cached Autoregressive Inference
items.summary: KV caching is ubiquitous in LLM inference, and showing deterministic FP16 divergence from cache-free decoding is broadly important for evaluation, deployment, and systems research.
items.spotlight_comment: KV caching is ubiquitous in LLM inference, and showing deterministic FP16 divergence from cache-free decoding is broadly important for evaluation, deployment, and systems research.

items.title: StoSignSGD: Unbiased Structural Stochasticity Fixes SignSGD for Training Large Language Models
items.summary: This is broadly important beyond a niche because it targets large-model training stability, sign optimizers, and FP8 pretraining speedups with both theory and large-scale empirical evidence.
items.spotlight_comment: This is broadly important beyond a niche because it targets large-model training stability, sign optimizers, and FP8 pretraining speedups with both theory and large-scale empirical evidence.

items.title: LACE: Lattice Attention for Cross-thread Exploration
items.summary: Collaborative inference across parallel reasoning threads is broadly timely and the architectural idea is prominent enough for the daily hotspot feed.
items.spotlight_comment: Collaborative inference across parallel reasoning threads is broadly timely and the architectural idea is prominent enough for the daily hotspot feed.

items.title: The Spectral Geometry of Thought: Phase Transitions, Instruction Reversal, Token-Level Dynamics, and Perfect Correctness Prediction in How Transformers Reason
items.summary: Mechanistic analyses of reasoning dynamics in LLM internals are broadly salient, and the strong correctness-prediction claim makes it day-relevant.
items.spotlight_comment: Mechanistic analyses of reasoning dynamics in LLM internals are broadly salient, and the strong correctness-prediction claim makes it day-relevant.
